rationale: The citing paper references Bilgin and LeBoeuf (2010) once in the background section as an example that anticipated interval length can vary with contextual valence (loss vs. gain endpoints). It is used to illustrate that prior marketing work has found biases in future time perception, alongside another citation, before introducing the paper's own categorization-based framework. The cited study is not used for hypothesis derivation, mechanism adoption, measurement, or empirical comparison later in the paper, so its role is a brief contextual mention rather than a core building block.
